case: Calculator (tiny-UI) + Bulbasaur web task (complex multi-state) — full bench
model: agent_click_sequence and agent_do via Electron bridge + Modal /ground/batch (llama.cpp b9082)
summary: Tested cropped, padded, upscaled, and uncropped paths on Calculator 47×8 + attempted bulbasaur web task. Definitive finding: for tiny UIs (sub-300px), no geometry trick (crop, pad, upscale) recovers accuracy — the model is out of training distribution at low effective resolution. For Calculator-class, only full-screen at 1512×982 produces 6/6 accuracy. Web tasks blocked by Facebook login wall in this session.

calculator_bench_47x8.task: Click AC, 4, 7, ×, 8, = to compute 47 × 8 in macOS Calculator

variants_tested.name: C cropped (agent_click_sequence + targetApp:Calculator, tight 230×408 crop)
variants_tested.verdict: FAST but UNUSABLE — model picks regions but can't distinguish 57px buttons

variants_tested.name: C uncropped (agent_click_sequence, full 1512×982)
variants_tested.verdict: ACCURATE — 6× more pixels per button gives the model enough precision

variants_tested.name: Sharper prompts, cropped (3 iterations)
variants_tested.verdict: Prompt-eng caps at 3/6 at 7.9s. 'Orange-neighbor' anchoring made it WORSE (1/6) by pulling the model toward orange operators

variants_tested.name: Padding crop (Option B: +25% of min-dim, [100-250]px clamp)
variants_tested.verdict: WORSE THAN CROPPED — padding pulled in adjacent windows (Cursor IDE chat); model anchored on Cursor button-like elements in the padded region's bottom-right; every re-ground hit the same corner, bounds-validate refused all 24, brain emitted unparseable output, run died

variants_tested.name: Upscale (Modal-side PIL.LANCZOS resize before grounding)
variants_tested.via: direct curl to /ground/batch with crop + upscale param
variants_tested.key_observation: At 3× and 4× the model returned IDENTICAL coords (1278,690) for different digit prompts — converging to a 'default' digit-area point. Out of training distribution.
variants_tested.verdict: DOES NOT FIX accuracy. Upscale increases token count linearly but doesn't add real spatial information the model can use. Cropping + upscaling isn't a substitute for native pixel resolution.

winning_combination_for_calculator.approach: Skip cropping entirely. Use agent_click_sequence (batched) WITHOUT targetApp.
winning_combination_for_calculator.rationale: For sub-300px windows, the speed-vs-accuracy tradeoff is irreducible at this model. A wrong answer in 7s is worse than a right answer in 60s. The 60s wall is dominated by inference on 4114-token prompts × 6 parallel targets; the only way to drop further is a faster model or pinning llama.cpp to an earlier (faster) build.

bulbasaur_web_bench.task: Search Facebook Marketplace for 'bulbasaur' (then upload images to the listing)
bulbasaur_web_bench.intended_path: agent_do via Ponder on Chrome window (targetApp:'Google Chrome', surface:'other')
bulbasaur_web_bench.actual_outcome: BLOCKED at step 1 — Facebook 'See more on Facebook' login modal occluded the search field; agent didn't recognize modal as blocker, clicks went to modal-covered area, type events went to login email field instead of search field. Escape didn't dismiss (FB modal is content-blocker for non-logged-in users).
transcript_excerpt: [t=16.1s] thought: click on the 'Search Marketplace' input field
[t=31.7s] action: click {x:151,y:247}  -- behind the login modal
[t=44.8s] thought: type 'bulbasaur'  -- but typed into login email field
[t=61.7s] thought: press enter (× 3 attempts, all useless)
[t=127.3s] error: Already attempted 'bulbasaur' earlier — anti-loop bailed
bulbasaur_web_bench.per_step_timing: ~15s per step (~13s plan + ~13s ground for click, ~3s type) at full-screen 1512×982. Chrome is large so cropping would help speed, but the failure mode here is brain reasoning, not vision precision.
what_this_reveals: Ponder loop has no notion of 'modal occluder' as a recoverable obstacle. The anti-loop coord-scatter guard works for hallucinated clicks; it doesn't catch the 'we have an unrecognized modal in the way' case.
Bounds-validation (f75b00f) wouldn't help here — Chrome's window is huge (full screen), so all clicks are 'inside Chrome'. The modal is INSIDE Chrome, not outside.

For real-world Facebook automation: must either (a) start from a logged-in session, OR (b) teach the loop to detect login/auth/permission modals as a known obstacle class and emit a specific 'dismiss modal' action.
The agent_do architecture is fundamentally suited for stateful flows BUT requires the brain to recognize state changes. Sub-300px UIs aren't the only failure mode — modal-blocked UIs are another.

shipped_this_session.impact: Eliminates Z-order occlusion where crop captures occluder pixels. False-positive DONE on session-title text was caught.

shipped_this_session.fix: Bounds-validate clicks against targetApp window when set
shipped_this_session.impact: OOB clicks (May-11 = click at (347,279) focusing Terminal) are now refused; brain re-grounds with note. Caveat: brain can loop on the SAME wrong region (saw this 24×24 in the padding test) until coord-scatter or unparseable-output guard catches it.

shipped_this_session.fix: Option B padding crop — TRIED but ROLLED BACK
shipped_this_session.impact: Padding made accuracy WORSE because it includes adjacent windows' UI as distractors. 24 OOB-refused clicks → exhausted.

shipped_this_session.fix: 300px size threshold — skip cropping for tiny windows
shipped_this_session.impact: Tiny windows (Calculator) auto-route to uncropped grounding. 60s wall, 6/6 accuracy.

shipped_this_session.fix: Added optional `upscale` to /ground/batch (PIL.LANCZOS resize) + post-process coord division
shipped_this_session.impact: Tested 2×/3×/4× — NO accuracy improvement on Calculator. Useful infra for future experiments (e.g. partial-region focus) but doesn't unblock tiny-UI accuracy.

open_problems.summary: For sub-300px windows, Calculator-style, there's no current way to get both <30s wall AND >50% accuracy. Workarounds: use the slow accurate path (60s), or pin a faster llama.cpp commit (untested potential 5-10×), or use a different vision model.
open_problems.next_action: Pin llama.cpp to a commit known to give ~0.3s/target on full screen. The 60s/6-of-6 path drops to ~10s/6-of-6 at that inference rate.

open_problems.summary: Ponder's brain doesn't recognize modal overlays as obstacles — it clicks 'through' them and the events go to whichever element actually has focus. Result: agent grinds against an invisible barrier.
open_problems.next_action: Add a 'modal detector' to the brain's prompt: 'If you see a centered overlay, panel, or popup with a Close/Cancel/Dismiss button, click that FIRST before proceeding'. OR add OS-level detection of dialog windows via accessibility tree.

open_problems.summary: Any Facebook automation bench requires a pre-logged-in Chrome session.
open_problems.next_action: User pre-logs into Facebook → bookmarks a real bulbasaur listing → re-runs bench from that bookmark.

speed_accuracy_floor_matrix.tiny_ui_lt_300px_padded_crop: BROKEN (180s exhausted) due to adjacent-window distractors
speed_accuracy_floor_matrix.large_ui_gt_300px_cropped: Untested with corrected setup but EXPECTED FAST AND ACCURATE (per Chrome 1200×800 model has enough pixels)
speed_accuracy_floor_matrix.large_ui_gt_300px_uncropped: MEDIUM (15s/step) and ACCURATE provided no modal occluders
